name: Nerve Blocks & Injections
description: Greater occipital nerve blocks, sphenopalatine ganglion blocks, botulinum toxin injections. Procedural interventions for refractory cases.

all_trials.title: Botulinum Toxin Type A Blockade of the Sphenopalatine Ganglion in Treatment-refractory Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.sponsor: Norwegian University of Science and Technology
all_trials.what_tested: Botulinum toxin A (Botox) injected precisely into the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) using CT-guided navigation, to reduce attack frequency in treatment-resistant chronic CH.
all_trials.key_result: Still recruiting 112 participants, expected completion September 2025. Earlier phase 1/2 data suggested the approach was safe and beneficial.
all_trials.patient_relevance: If Botox injected at the SPG reduces chronic CH attacks, it could offer months of relief from a single injection for patients who have failed medications.
all_trials.dose_tested: Botulinum toxin A single injection to sphenopalatine ganglion

all_trials.title: Clinical Trial of Endoscopically Guided Injection of Exparel (Bupivacaine) for the Treatment of Craniofacial Pain
all_trials.what_tested: Endoscopic injection of long-acting bupivacaine (Exparel) near the sphenopalatine ganglion for craniofacial pain including cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Still recruiting 15 participants, expected completion June 2025.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A long-acting nerve block that could provide up to 72 hours of pain relief from a single injection -- early-stage and small but interesting.
all_trials.dose_tested: Exparel (long-acting bupivacaine) endoscopic injection to lateral nasal wall

all_trials.title: Occipital Nerve Stimulation in Medically Intractable Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Occipital nerve stimulation (ONS) via implanted electrodes at the back of the head, comparing high (100%) vs low (30%) stimulation intensity for treatment-resistant chronic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 130 participants but no results posted. This was the largest randomized controlled trial of ONS for chronic CH.
all_trials.patient_relevance: ONS is already used clinically for refractory CH in some countries -- this major trial should clarify how effective it really is, but results are not yet public.
all_trials.dose_tested: Occipital nerve stimulation at 100% vs 30% intensity

all_trials.title: SPG Pulsed Radiofrequency for Chronic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency (PRF) targeting the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) as a minimally invasive treatment for chronic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Recruiting 108 participants, expected completion December 2029.
all_trials.patient_relevance: SPG pulsed radiofrequency is a minimally invasive procedure that avoids permanent nerve damage -- this large trial could establish it as a standard option for chronic CH.
all_trials.dose_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency to sphenopalatine ganglion

all_trials.title: Pulsed Radiofrequency Combined With Platelet-Rich Plasma Applied to the Sphenopalatine Ganglion in Treating Episodic Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency combined with platelet-rich plasma (PRP) injected at the sphenopalatine ganglion for episodic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Not yet recruiting, expected to enroll 242 participants starting May 2025.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Combining two minimally invasive techniques could potentially extend pain relief duration -- a novel approach worth watching.
all_trials.dose_tested: Pulsed radiofrequency + platelet-rich plasma at SPG

all_trials.title: Botulinum Toxin Type A Block of the Otic Ganglion in Chronic Cluster Headache: Safety Issues
all_trials.sponsor: Norwegian University of Science and Technology
all_trials.what_tested: Safety of Botox injected into the otic ganglion (a different nerve cluster than the usual SPG target) in chronic cluster headache patients.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 10 participants. This was a phase 1/2 safety pilot testing 12.5 and 25 IU botulinum toxin. No results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Early safety study exploring a new injection target for CH -- too small to tell if it works, but establishes whether the approach is safe.
all_trials.dose_tested: 12.5 IU and 25 IU botulinum toxin A to otic ganglion

all_trials.title: Botox Injection in Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.sponsor: Norwegian University of Science and Technology
all_trials.what_tested: Safety of Botox injected into the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) using CT-guided navigation for treatment-resistant cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 10 participants. This was a phase 1/2 safety and feasibility study testing 25 IU and 50 IU doses. No results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This Norwegian pilot established the safety of SPG Botox injection for CH, paving the way for the larger phase 3 trial (NCT03944876).
all_trials.dose_tested: 25 IU and 50 IU botulinum toxin A to sphenopalatine ganglion

all_trials.title: Occipital Nerve Stimulation to Treat Chronic Headaches
all_trials.what_tested: A tiny implantable nerve stimulator (bion microstimulator) placed near the greater occipital nerve to treat chronic headaches including cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 20 participants but no results posted. This was one of the earliest occipital nerve stimulation studies for headache.
all_trials.patient_relevance: An early study of implantable nerve stimulation for chronic headache -- the technology has evolved significantly since this 2004-2006 trial.
all_trials.dose_tested: Bion microstimulator implanted at greater occipital nerve

all_trials.title: Sphenopalatine Ganglion Stimulation for the Acute Treatment of Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: An implanted neurostimulator to electrically stimulate the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) for acute treatment of cluster headache attacks.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 43 participants. The Pathway CH-1 study showed SPG stimulation provided pain relief in 67% of attacks and complete pain freedom in 34% within 15 minutes.
all_trials.patient_relevance: SPG stimulation was highly effective for aborting CH attacks -- this led to the Pulsante device being available in Europe, though the company later closed.
all_trials.dose_tested: SPG neurostimulation via implanted ATI device

all_trials.title: Data Collection on the ATI Neurostimulation System: SPG Stimulation for Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Post-market registry collecting real-world data on the ATI SPG neurostimulation system implant for cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 150 participants. No formal results posted, but this registry supported the commercial use of the Pulsante device in Europe.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Real-world data collection on the SPG stimulator -- the device showed promise but the company (Autonomic Technologies) eventually closed down.
all_trials.dose_tested: ATI Neurostimulation System (SPG stimulator)

all_trials.title: Sphenopalatine Ganglion Pulsed Radiofrequency Treatment Outcomes
all_trials.what_tested: Retrospective evaluation of sphenopalatine ganglion pulsed radiofrequency treatment outcomes for chronic headaches and facial pain, tracking pain scores over 6 months.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 55 participants but no results posted yet. Data collected at 1 week, 1 month, 3 months, and 6 months.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Real-world outcomes data on SPG radiofrequency could help patients decide whether this minimally invasive procedure is worth trying.
all_trials.dose_tested: Fluoroscopy-guided SPG pulsed radiofrequency

all_trials.title: The Efficacy and Safety of Sphenopalatine Ganglion Pulsed Radiofrequency Treatment for Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Sphenopalatine ganglion pulsed radiofrequency for treating cluster headache -- effectiveness and safety.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 80 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A reasonably large study of SPG radiofrequency for CH from a major Chinese center -- results would be valuable but have not been published.
all_trials.dose_tested: Sphenopalatine ganglion pulsed radiofrequency

all_trials.title: Quantitative Sensory Testing and Occipital Nerve Stimulator
all_trials.what_tested: Whether occipital nerve stimulator (ONS) implantation changes pain processing and biomarkers in chronic migraine and cluster headache patients.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 6 participants. This was a mechanistic study comparing pain perception before and after ONS implantation.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Understanding how ONS changes pain processing could help predict which patients will respond -- basic science, not a treatment trial.
all_trials.dose_tested: Occipital nerve stimulator (implanted)

all_trials.title: Two US-guided Techniques for Greater Occipital Nerve Blocks
all_trials.what_tested: Comparing two ultrasound-guided techniques for greater occipital nerve (GON) blocks -- a proximal approach at the C2 vertebra versus a distal approach at the base of the skull.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 40 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: How your doctor performs a GON block may affect how well it works -- this study compared two common injection sites.
all_trials.dose_tested: GON block with local anesthetic and steroid, two different injection sites

all_trials.title: SPG Neurostimulation in Cluster Patients
all_trials.what_tested: Whether low-frequency electrical stimulation of the sphenopalatine ganglion (SPG) can trigger cluster-like attacks by activating parasympathetic nerves.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 21 participants but no results posted. This was a provocation study to understand CH mechanisms.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Basic science study to understand how the SPG contributes to CH attacks -- helps explain why SPG-targeted treatments work.
all_trials.dose_tested: Low-frequency SPG stimulation (provocation study)

all_trials.title: Pathway CH-1 Long-Term Follow-Up
all_trials.what_tested: Long-term follow-up of patients from the Pathway CH-1 trial who had SPG neurostimulators implanted for cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 33 participants. This tracked the safety and effectiveness of SPG stimulation over years of use.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Long-term data on SPG stimulation is critical for patients considering an implant -- suggests the device continues to work over time.
all_trials.dose_tested: ATI SPG Neurostimulation System (long-term follow-up)

all_trials.title: Cephalic Vascular Recording Upon SPG Stimulation
all_trials.what_tested: How SPG stimulation at different frequencies affects blood flow in the brain -- high frequency was expected to block parasympathetic activity, low frequency to activate it.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 7 participants. This was a mechanistic study measuring blood vessel responses to different stimulation patterns.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Understanding how SPG stimulation changes blood flow helps explain why it can both abort attacks and prevent them -- important basic science.
all_trials.dose_tested: SPG stimulation at various frequencies (1-200 Hz)

all_trials.title: Treatment of Chronic Cluster Headache with TENS and ONS
all_trials.what_tested: A three-phase study testing TENS (transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation), then randomizing to either burst occipital nerve stimulation or sham, then open-label conventional ONS for chronic cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 38 participants but no results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This study tested both non-invasive (TENS) and implanted (ONS) nerve stimulation for chronic CH -- results could help decide between these options.
all_trials.dose_tested: TENS then burst ONS vs sham ONS then tonic ONS

all_trials.title: Intra-arterial Sphenopalatine Ganglion Block for Patients With Refractory Headache
all_trials.sponsor: Weill Medical College of Cornell University
all_trials.what_tested: Intra-arterial delivery of dexamethasone and ketorolac into the arteries feeding the sphenopalatine ganglion for refractory headaches including cluster headache.
all_trials.key_result: Withdrawn before enrolling any participants. The study never got off the ground.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This novel approach of delivering medication directly to the SPG through blood vessels was never tested -- the concept remains unproven.
all_trials.dose_tested: Intra-arterial dexamethasone + ketorolac to SPG arteries

all_trials.title: Bilateral Occipital Nerve Field Stimulation for the Treatment of dtCCH
all_trials.what_tested: A novel bilateral occipital nerve field stimulation device (BliStim) for preventing chronic cluster headaches -- first-in-human safety study.
all_trials.key_result: Suspended with 10 participants enrolled. Reasons for suspension not stated.
all_trials.patient_relevance: A new type of nerve stimulation device for CH, but the study was suspended -- unclear if it will resume.
all_trials.dose_tested: BliStim bilateral occipital nerve field stimulation
